faqs.q: Why does avoidance make anxiety worse?
faqs.a: Avoidance brings instant relief, so the urge to avoid grows stronger next time. Because the person never sticks around to learn the feared outcome doesn't happen, the fear never gets a chance to shrink, which keeps anxiety going.

faqs.q: What's the difference between avoidance and healthy caution?
faqs.a: Healthy caution protects you from real danger, like not walking down a dark alley alone. Avoidance protects you from feeling afraid even when there's no real danger.
A clinician can help, often with approaches like exposure therapy that gently reverse the cycle by helping a person face feared situations at a manageable pace.
